article_title: Samsung Unveils First HBM5 Mockup at Computex 2026, Putting Thermal Management at the Center of the Next AI Memory Race
summary: Samsung HBM5 main story fully verified; one embellished quote in the secondary SK hynix context ('more than 30%' vs the source's '30%', plus added 'electrically'/'thermally conductive' qualifiers) filed as a correction.

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)
Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

Non-verbatim/embellished quote in the SK hynix paragraph. The article quotes iHBM as using 'cooling elements made of electrically non-conductive, thermally conductive silicon into the D2D PHY layer, which the company said cuts thermal resistance by more than 30% against current products'. The cited Tom's Hardware snapshot says 'non-conductive silicon cooling elements' in the D2D PHY layer and 'cut thermal resistance by 30%' (the source's own headline and URL say 'by 30%', not 'more than 30%').
findings.details: Recoverable: the substance is accurate (~30% thermal-resistance reduction, non-conductive silicon, D2D PHY), but the quoted wording adds 'electrically'/'thermally conductive' and inflates '30%' to 'more than 30%'. Confined to the secondary SK hynix context, not the Samsung HBM5 headline/lead. Filed as a corrections record.

content_preview.title: Samsung Unveils First HBM5 Mockup at Computex 2026, Putting Thermal Management at the Center of the Next AI Memory Race
content_preview.summary: At Computex 2026, Samsung showed its first HBM5 mockup with a 2nm base die and Heat Path Block cooling, with mass production not expected before 2028.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Samsung used Computex 2026 in Taipei to show a physical mockup of its eighth-generation high-bandwidth memory, HBM5, for the first time, pairing it with a new in-package cooling structure, according to [Tom's Hardware](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/semiconductors/samsung-shows-first-hbm5-mockup-at-computex-with-heat-path-block-cooling). The disclosure was modest in form — a mockup rather than a shipping product — but its substance points to where the contest for AI memo...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight, well-framed semiconductor News piece (596 words) on Samsung's HBM5 mockup and the broader thermal-management contest, with all sources on the allowlist (the bot rebuilt the article on allowlisted outlets after dropping off-allowlist ones).
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200; two via Archive.org fallback). source-0 (Tom's Hardware, Samsung HBM5) confirms verbatim 'eighth-generation', 'Heat Path Block', the HPB thermal-pillars quote, the 'concentrates on the D2D PHY layer… increase exponentially' quote, the '2nm … down from the 4nm node used for HBM4 and HBM4E' quote, and the before-2028 timing. source-1 (TrendForce) confirms the 12/16/20-layer stack options, the 'an independent thermal path within the D2D PHY region' quote, and the ~2028 production window. source-2 (Tom's Hardware, SK hynix iHBM) confirms the 'from next-generation products, such as HBM5' quote verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The Samsung HBM5 story — every quote and specific (Heat Path Block, D2D PHY, 2nm base die, stack options, 2028) — is verbatim and fully verified. One issue in the secondary SK hynix paragraph: the article's quote 'cooling elements made of electrically non-conductive, thermally conductive silicon … cuts thermal resistance by more than 30% against current products' is not verbatim — the cited snapshot says 'non-conductive silicon cooling elements' and 'cut thermal resistance by 30%' (the source's headline and URL both say 'by 30%', not 'more than 30%'). The substance is accurate; the wording was embellished and the figure inflated. Filed as a correction.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; 'What We Don't Know' correctly flags the mockup (non-product) status and the un-committed 2028 window.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior HBM5 or Samsung/SK hynix thermal-architecture coverage in the archive.
concerns: One embellished quote in the SK hynix context ('more than 30%' vs the source's '30%') — filed as a correction rather than a rejection, since the headline/lead and the entire Samsung HBM5 story are verbatim-accurate.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate; APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS for the single embellished SK hynix quote.
